content.speakerText: This seems super niche—how’s this useful in real classrooms?
content.speakerTextCn: 这看起来太小众了，实际课堂能用吗？

options.text: It’s only for elite schools with lots of funding.
Actually, it’s designed for under-resourced schools—we’ve piloted it in 3 districts with promising results.
I don’t know, maybe it’s not useful at all.
We’re planning to expand it to universities next year.

content.explanation: 正确答案直接回应了质疑，用具体试点案例证明实用性，符合'用实例证明普适性'的使用说明。选项1是防御性反驳，与场景相反；选项3是消极回避；选项4虽积极但回应的是高等教育问题，而非原问题中的'实际课堂'。

content.speakerText: Could this be applied in higher ed? Your data’s all from high school.
content.speakerTextCn: 这个能用在高等教育吗？你的数据全是高中阶段的。

options.text: No, it’s only meant for teenagers.
We’re planning a follow-up study with undergrads next semester—core principles transfer well.
I think you should do your own research if you’re interested.
High school data is more reliable anyway.

content.explanation: 正确答案展示了研究的延展性并表达开放态度，符合使用说明。选项1是绝对否定，缺乏建设性；选项3是推卸责任；选项4贬低对方观点，不符合专业回应原则。

content.speakerText: This intervention sounds too theoretical—has anyone actually tried it outside the lab?
content.speakerTextCn: 这个干预措施听起来太理论化了——有人在实验室之外试过吗？

options.text: It’s just a model—no one can implement it in real life.
It’s been used in 5 community colleges with improved student participation rates.
Maybe if you had better funding, it would work.
We haven’t thought about real-world use yet.

content.explanation: 正确答案用具体���施案例回应质疑，体现实践价值。选项1和4是消极否认，选项2是合理回应，选项3是归咎外部条件，不符合'避免防御性回应'的原则。

content.template: Actually, it’s designed for ______ schools—we’ve piloted it in 3 districts with promising results.
content.hint: 这是回应'小众无用'质疑时的关键描述，强调服务对象的现实需求
content.knowledgePoint: 场景高频词汇（资源匮乏学校）

content.template: Maybe not directly—but the ______ model? Totally adaptable.
content.hint: 这是回应应用范围质疑时的核心概念，指学生参与方式而非具体内容
content.knowledgePoint: 场景核心句型（表达可迁移性）

content.hint: 这是简洁有力的回应，强调该方法在大型项目失效时的优势
content.knowledgePoint: 场景惯用搭配（big programs fail）

content.speakerText: This seems super niche—how’s this useful in real classrooms?
content.speakerTextCn: 这看起来太小众了，实际课堂能用吗？
expectedAnswers: It’s designed for under-resourced schools—we’ve seen improved student engagement in pilot districts.
Even though it seems niche, it fills a gap that big programs ignore—teachers in rural areas are using it successfully.
It’s not about being broad; it’s about being effective where support is lacking.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否直接回应了对实用性的质疑
语言准确性：语法正确，用词恰当，无中式英语
表达自然度：是否使用了自然口语表达（如we’ve seen, fills a gap）
关键词覆盖：是否包含 under-resourced / pilot / effective 等场景关键词

content.speakerText: Could this be applied in higher ed? Your data’s all from high school.
content.speakerTextCn: 这个能用在高等教育吗？你的数据全是高中阶段的。
expectedAnswers: We’re planning a follow-up study with undergrads next semester—the core principles are transferable across age groups.
Not yet, but the way it boosts student motivation could definitely be adapted for college settings.
The framework isn’t grade-specific—it’s about engagement patterns, which exist in both high school and university.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否承认当前限制但展示未来可能性
语言准确性：正确使用 transferable / adaptable / framework 等术语
表达自然度：是否避免绝对化表达（如 'never' 或 'only'），体现开放态度
关键词覆盖：是否包含 follow-up / transferable / adaptable / framework

content.speakerText: This approach seems too simple to make a real difference—what’s the evidence?
content.speakerTextCn: 这种方法太简单了，怎么可能产生真正影响？有什么证据吗？
expectedAnswers: Simplicity is intentional—we found that complex tools aren’t used consistently. Our data shows a 30% increase in participation with minimal training.
It’s not about complexity—it’s about accessibility. Teachers using this reported higher retention rates over six months.
The evidence is in the outcomes: schools with limited budgets saw the biggest improvements, which is exactly why this works.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否用数据或结果回应'太简单无效'的质疑
语言准确性：使用正确时态和学术表达（e.g., reported, saw, shows）
表达自然度：是否避免过度学术化，保持口语化但专业
关键词覆盖：是否包含 evidence / data / participation / retention / limited budgets
